title: Translation provider expansion
goal: Allow Vocab Flow users to use direct Gemini and Claude/Anthropic BYO APIs, keep OpenAI-compatible gateways for cheap multi-provider routing, and reduce avoidable google-free requests without changing the review-first vocabulary workflow.
constraints: No provider is enabled by default.
Never send source context unless the user enables the provider-specific context toggle.
Keep google-free framed as inaccurate, unofficial, and review-only.
Do not require remote CI or paid provider calls for verification.

changes: dedupe repeated terms per translation run
cache successful terms inside the translator instance

providers.role: gateway for OpenAI, OpenRouter, LiteLLM, local LLM proxies, and other Chat Completions-compatible endpoints
providers.contextPolicy: provider setting controls term-only vs term-plus-context
provider setting controls term-only vs term-plus-context
provider setting controls term-only vs term-plus-context

implementationSlices.name: translator core
tests: create Gemini translator from prefs and parse JSON response
create Anthropic translator from prefs and parse text block JSON response
store provider-specific prefs and provider id together
dedupe google-free duplicate terms in one run

implementationSlices.name: menu wiring
tests: register Gemini and Claude/Anthropic configuration menu items
save Gemini settings from menu configuration
save Anthropic settings from menu configuration

edgeCases: Duplicate terms should not create duplicate google-free network calls.
Provider response JSON inside Markdown fences should still parse.
Unknown provider pref values must fall back to off.
Missing API key, model, or endpoint must return no translations without network calls.
Provider output keys not exactly matching requested terms must be ignored.
Partial google-free failures must keep earlier successful translations.
Context text may contain quotes/newlines and must be JSON encoded inside the prompt payload.
User cancellation in menu prompts must not alter saved provider prefs.

holesAndMitigations.mitigation: Keep request builders small and covered by shape tests; document OpenAI-compatible gateways as the most flexible path.

holesAndMitigations.hole: No live Gemini/Anthropic calls in CI because API keys are user-owned.
holesAndMitigations.mitigation: Mock exact request/response shapes locally and leave manual smoke testing as release evidence.

holesAndMitigations.hole: google-free is unofficial and can rate-limit or block.
holesAndMitigations.mitigation: Avoid concurrency by default; only eliminate duplicate requests and keep warnings visible.

holesAndMitigations.hole: Provider keys are stored in Zotero preferences.
holesAndMitigations.mitigation: Warn before configuration and never commit or log keys.

doneSignals: Tests cover each new provider adapter and menu path.
Docs explain direct Gemini/Claude, OpenAI-compatible gateways, and google-free limits.
Local unit tests, typecheck, and build pass.
